title: Result - Lithuania
content:
Welcome to Lithuania, a safe and welcoming country known for its multilingual and multicultural diversity. A member of NATO, the EU and the Schengen area, Lithuania offers a mild climate with average temperatures of -5°C in January and 17°C in July.
Explore national parks, kayak serene rivers, bike coastal trails, or admire the Baltic Sea’s dramatic sand dunes. Lithuania’s rich history, dating to the 12th century, is reflected in its art, architecture and cuisine. Savor traditional dishes like cepelinai, šaltibarščiai and rye bread. With a growing GDP, Lithuania thrives in fields like financial tech, robotics, lasers and nanotechnology.

## Unique food tradition
Kugelis (potato casserole): made with potatoes, bacon, eggs, milk, onions and seasonings and then baked in the oven.
As a country with a strong focus on education, Lithuania has a growing demand for educators, particularly English teachers.

### Local legend or myth
Eglė (the Queen of Serpents): a young woman is transformed into a tree after her marriage to a serpent king.

### Language of the Land
Lithuanian is one of the oldest languages in the world, with a linguistic history of 5,000 years.
lakes are scattered across Lithuania, the largest being Lake Drūkšiai, which covers around 44 square kilometres.
Kaziuko Mugė (St. Casimir’s Fair)
